summary:
Iteration 6 verified end-to-end. Backend: 118/118 pytest PASS (49 regression + 50 prior CRM + 19 new v6 covering summary/aggregates/sync/non-GST/new filters/convert). Frontend: all new CRM pages render with the expected testids, headers, KPI cards and vibrant StatusPill styling.

New backend coverage (test_crm_v6.py): (1) /leads/summary returns {total, by_stage, pipeline_value, won_value, follow_ups_due_7d}; /vendors/summary returns {total, active, inactive, total_paid_outflow}; /projects/summary returns full spec keys; /reachvel-payments/summary returns {total, credit_total, debit_total, net, synced, manual}. (2) New filters: leads ?service= + ?source=, vendors ?status= (active includes legacy records missing the field), projects ?project_group=, reachvel-payments ?category=&bank=&project_id=. (3) GST off ⇒ gst_amount=0, gst_pct=0, total=amount on POST and PUT for invoices/expenses/vendor-payments; flipping GST back on recomputes correctly. (4) Project list now includes per-row total_expenses (gen_exp_total + paid_vendor_exp_total). (5) /projects/aggregates returns spec-compliant per-project rollup (total_budget/total_invoiced/total_received/total_general_expenses/total_vendor_expenses/profit/status). (6) /reachvel-payments/sync materializes 1 credit per project_payment + 1 debit per expense + 1 debit per paid vendor_payment, second call returns added_credits=0 added_debits=0 (idempotent). Materialized rows carry source_type ∈ {project_payment, expense, vendor_payment}; manual creates carry source_type='manual'. (7) POST /leads/{id}/convert produces a project with project_group='', gst_applicable=true, vendor_id='', budget=lead.value.

Frontend UI verified (live preview): admin login with reachvel-admin-2026 OK; Leads page shows 4 KPI cards (Total Leads / Pipeline Value / Won Deals Value / Follow-ups 7D), Stage+Service+Source filters, and table headers '#|Name|Company|Email|Phone|Service|Source|Value|Stage|Owner|Follow-up|Created|Updated|Convert|Actions'; status pills are vibrant (QUALIFIED purple, CONTACTED blue, WON green — background tint + border + dot). Vendors page shows TOTAL/ACTIVE/INACTIVE KPIs. Projects page shows all 8 KPI cards and table columns 'Group | GST | … | Expenses'. Project Payments page exposes data-testid crm-project-payments-page + crm-pp-refresh and the EXACT 10 spec columns 'S.No|Project|GST|Total Budget|Total Invoiced|Total Received|Gen. Expenses|Vendor Expenses|Profit|Status'. Reachvel Payments page exposes data-testid crm-reachvel-sync, has Bank/Source/Project/Origin columns + Type/Category/Bank filters + 4 KPI cards (Total Credit/Total Debit/Net Cashflow/Records with '7 synced · 1 manual'). Clicking SYNC PROJECTS triggers /sync and shows toast 'Already up to date.' (idempotent path). All ledger pills (DEBIT/CREDIT/SYNCED/MANUAL/INFRA/SOFTWARE/CLIENT PAYMENT/PROFESSIONAL FEES/SALARY) render with colorful tinted backgrounds + borders.
critical_code_review_comments: crm_routes.py update endpoints for /vendor-payments, /invoices, /expenses do NOT re-validate the status enum on PUT (only POST). A PUT with status='banana' would persist silently. Same gap exists for /tasks status & priority. Recommend adding the same enum guards used in create_* to the update_* handlers (carried over from iteration_5 — still unfixed).

_apply_gst lives inside the generic _create/_update via an `if collection_name in GST_COLLECTIONS` branch. Cleaner to pass an optional `pre_save` hook or move the call to the route handlers themselves to keep the helpers truly generic.
/projects/aggregates does N+1 Mongo finds (1 list of projects + 4 finds per project). At ~50+ projects this becomes wasteful; consider a single $lookup/$facet pipeline if the project count grows.

/reachvel-payments/sync `_upsert` checks for 'changed' fields with a generic loop but only sets {'$set': doc} — `doc` is the local dict containing ONLY the data fields, not id/source_type/source_id. That is correct, but on update it does NOT bump created_at/keeps source_type/source_id intact (good). Just ensure if a sync record is manually edited later, the next sync call would overwrite those edits with the source's values — by-design, but worth a doc note.
Frontend sidebar nav testids follow `admin-nav-crm-<key>` (e.g. admin-nav-crm-leads). Spec/agent context referred to them as 'admin-nav-leads' — minor doc inconsistency, not a bug.
test_credentials: Admin password 'reachvel-admin-2026' — verified on live preview during UI run.
seed_data_creation: test_crm_v6.py creates TEST6-prefixed leads/vendors/projects/invoices/expenses/vendor_payments/project_payments/reachvel_payments and deletes them in test_cleanup methods. Sync test cleans the 3 materialized reachvel_payments by their source_id. After test run, two stale rows from a prior UI iteration (TEST UI Lead Alpha, etc.) were observed in the live UI but they're TEST-prefixed and harmless.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run all backend tests with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=https://reachvel-studio.preview.emergentagent.com python -m pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. There are now 4 backend test files (backend_test.py + test_cms.py + test_crm.py + test_crm_v6.py) totalling 118 tests / ~28s. Sidebar admin-nav testids use the pattern admin-nav-crm-<key> (crm-leads, crm-vendors, crm-projects, crm-tasks, crm-project-payments, crm-reachvel-payments, crm-analytics). The Reachvel Payments sync endpoint is idempotent — running it twice yields 0/0 added on the second call. test_crm_v6 does NOT exhaustively cover convert idempotency or sync's 'updated' counter when underlying data changes; flag if needed.
rca of the issue: No defects found. The iteration 6 spec is fully implemented: all 4 summary endpoints, /projects/aggregates, /projects list total_expenses, /reachvel-payments/sync (idempotent + correct source_type), GST=false math, new filter params (leads service+source, vendors status, projects project_group, reachvel-payments category+bank+project_id), and the convert→project new-field defaults. Frontend renders the spec-mandated 10-column Project Payments aggregate table, the 8-card Projects KPI grid, the 4-card Leads KPI grid, the Reachvel Payments Sync Projects button + toast, and vibrant StatusPill backgrounds across every status surface checked. Forward-looking code-quality notes captured under critical_code_review_comments.
